results.title: Reliance Industries Q1 Results: O2C business delivers four-year high EBITDA
results.description: Reliance Industries' oil-to-chemicals (O2C) business reported its highest quarterly EBITDA in four years, driven by a sharp recovery in transportation fuel cracks and stronger downstream chemical margins.

results.title: ConocoPhillips to Acquire 42% Stake in BP's Kirkuk Oil Venture in Iraq
results.description: ConocoPhillips is strengthening its presence in Iraq's energy sector after reaching an agreement to acquire a 42 per cent stake in BP Energy Company of Kirkuk Limited (BP ECKL), a move expected to accelerate the redevelopment of some of northern Iraq's largest producing oil fields. The acquisition forms part of broader efforts to attract international investment into Iraq's oil and gas industry while enhancing production capacity and reducing dependence on export routes exposed to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The agreement is expected to be formally signed during Iraqi Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi's official visit to Washington, D.C., this week, highlighting growing energy cooperation between Iraq and the United States. Commenting on the transaction, ConocoPhillips Chief Executive Officer, Ryan Lance, said the investment aligns with the company's long-term strategy of pursuing high-quality assets capable of delivering sustainable returns. “This unique redevelopment opportunity is well aligned with our disciplined investment framework, providing access to a material, high-quality and long-life resource base, comfortably meeting our cost of supply threshold.” The company said the transaction is expected to be completed before the end of the year, subject to regulatory approvals and the fulfilment of other customary closing conditions. BP Energy Company of Kirkuk Limited currently holds the development and production contract for the Baba and Avanah domes of the Kirkuk oil field, in addition to the neighbouring Bai Hassan, Jambur and Khabbaz fields. Collectively, these assets represent one of Iraq's most significant oil-producing regions and are expected to play a key role in the country's future energy expansion. BP Chief Executive Officer, Meg O'Neill, described the Kirkuk redevelopment project as strategically important for both Iraq and the company. “Kirkuk is a world-class resource base that can support Iraq’s long-term energy ambitions while creating value for both the country and bp.” The agreement comes as the United States intensifies efforts to encourage greater investment in Iraq's energy sector, with the objective of increasing crude oil production, strengthening energy security and diversifying export routes amid continuing regional instability. Energy markets have remained focused on the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most important oil transit routes, following renewed tensions between the United States and Iran. Before the outbreak of the regional conflict, the strategic waterway carried roughly one-fifth of global oil supplies, making supply diversification a priority for producers and consuming nations alike. BP has maintained operations in Iraq for nearly a century and has long been involved in the development of some of the country's largest oil fields, including the giant Rumaila field. In 2025, the company concluded an agreement with the Iraqi government to redevelop oil and gas resources in the Kirkuk region, covering the Baba and Avanah domes and the nearby Bai Hassan, Jambur and Khabbaz fields. The latest investment also reflects Iraq's ongoing efforts to attract leading international energy companies as it seeks to increase oil and gas output while accelerating the development of its natural gas resources. As part of those efforts, Prime Minister Al-Zaidi met with executives from Halliburton, Shell, Honeywell, Weatherford and Baker Hughes during a visit to Houston on Thursday. According to his office, discussions focused on new investment opportunities, technology transfer and potential collaboration on major energy infrastructure projects across Iraq.

results.title: India-EU Investment Protection Pact, GI agreements work in progress: Goyal
results.description: New Delhi: After concluding a free trade agreement, India and the European Union (EU) are working on the proposed investment protection and GI (Geographical Indications) agreements, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said on Friday. He said that the trade pacts will open huge opportunities for businesses of both sides. Following trade pacts with the [...]

results.title: People in Pune watch the Mutha River in spate after water was released from the Khadakwasla Dam on July 8
results.description: Maharashtra's reservoirs are at 44.45 per cent of their usable storage capacity, compared to 60.54 per cent last year.
